title: Posthuman Saga - Humans vs. Mutants in a struggle for survival!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2qZb4PtGQUg

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a quick look at **Posthuman Saga**, a competitive post-apocalyptic adventure-survival game, and frames it around five talking points. At its core it's a victory-point race across 16 rounds: each player builds their own quadrant of map from a hand of terrain cards, treks out to complete evolving missions, places scavenge tokens to unlock recon bonuses, and battles mutants.

He rates the card play a clear step up from the original Posthuman: Survive & Evolve — you choose actions (trek, forage, map, camp) as cards, and combat uses a "primary" challenge card plus drawn cards against the enemy's dice. The story-token encounters, quick but flavourful (and reactive to your visible mutations and followers), are among the game's best features. His sharpest criticism is the solo mode: themeless, with a punishingly strict timer, it did nothing for him — he recommends instead playing two-handed, rooting for one character against the other. He also flags the evolving-mission cards as a missed opportunity, since each mission always unfolds the same way. Affordable and still easy to find; he's slightly torn on whether he prefers it to the original.

----

Daniel found Posthuman Saga's official solo mode a letdown but loved playing it two-handed against himself. When a game's solo rules disappoint, do you play the multiplayer game solo instead — or just move on?
